title: fix(deps): update github.com/coreos/go-systemd/v22 digest to d5623bf
body:
[![Mend Renovate](https://app.renovatebot.com/images/banner.svg)](https://renovatebot.com)

This PR contains the following updates:

| Package | Type | Update | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| [github.com/coreos/go-systemd/v22](https://togithub.com/coreos/go-systemd) | require | digest | `7d375ec` -> `d5623bf` |

---

### Configuration

📅 **Schedule**: Branch creation - "after 1am and before 11am on the first day of the month" in timezone UTC, Automerge - At any time (no schedule defined).

🚦 **Automerge**: Disabled by config. Please merge this manually once you are satisfied.

♻ **Rebasing**: Never, or you tick the rebase/retry checkbox.

🔕 **Ignore**: Close this PR and you won't be reminded about this update again.

---

 - [ ] <!-- rebase-check -->If you want to rebase/retry this PR, check this box

---

This PR has been generated by [Mend Renovate](https://www.mend.io/free-developer-tools/renovate/). View repository job log [here](https://developer.mend.io/github/containers/podman).

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *renovate[bot]* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [zhangguanzhang](https://github.com/zhangguanzhang) for approval. For more information see [the Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Cockpit tests failed for commit bc570e701255fb00ebe361e57fff14507f190b01. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
OOI, what's the deal with this "renovate" bot that keeps opening and closing this PR? Is it flipping a coin or so? :thinking:

PR needs rebase.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

@cevich Mind looking at what is going on here?

As far as I can tell we are already vendored the latest commit so there should be no need need for an update. In fact is proposing a downgrade, then somehow realizing that it is already on the latest then auto closing and then rinse and repeat.

Whoa! Thanks for pointing this out to me.  My best guess is it's due to some activity in the coreos/go-systemd repo.  My only evidence for that is: We don't see this behavior elsewhere(?) :disappointed: 

I'll poke around in that repo. and see if there are any events that align with activity here...
There is no activity in that repo. The last commit is from me and was vendored in https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/ce334ed9dd2e149e30b6edb738417345ad33ac9e
oh crap, got distracted.  Thanks for checking.  What about on the release page?
Nope, last update (including artifacts) on release page was in Nov.  Hmmm.

I see two ways forward:

1. [Search for prior reports (any language) or ask for help as a new discussion](https://github.com/renovatebot/renovate/discussions).  This likely requires creating a reproducer in a dummy repo. (maybe not all that hard).
2. Add a config workaround.  Lots of options here, like forcing a very limited schedule for this specific package, or simply ignoring it entirely.  Maybe forcing it to ignore digests and only look at semantic release versions?

There is a third option. If renovate reopens the PR, close it manually. I think this makes it ignore updates for this version until it finds a newer one (which it shouldn't until upstream actually changes).

> until upstream actually changes

That's the thing I'm afraid might not work in this context.  But it's worth a try.  Please let me know if it doesn't fix the problem, and we can try one of the other things.  It wouldn't surprise me if there's a renovate bug here.  Upstream is normally really good about fixing them, especially if they're minimally reproducible.
re-opening so it can be manually closed (see above)
well damn.  it says the branch was deleted.

### Renovate Ignore Notification

Because you closed this PR without merging, Renovate will ignore this update. You will not get PRs for the `github.com/coreos/go-systemd/v22` `d5623bf` update again.

If you accidentally closed this PR, or if you changed your mind: rename this PR to get a fresh replacement PR.
There we go, good catch @Luap99.  I'm hopeful that will make it actually ignore this.
